mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals acute labor market deterioration, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances over a 5-year sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansions in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer demand growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint z-scores of RSAFS and HOUST show no predictive power (p > 0.10) for 60-day forward returns in regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects robust manufacturing output that supports corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive accuracy (Rank IC < 0.01) when GDPC1 growth exceeds 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and prompts tighter financial conditions, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in >50% of months where CPIAUCSL increases by more than 0.2%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 0.5% confirms economic stagnation, causing investors to price in recession risks and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Market returns remain positive in >65% of quarters where GDPC1 growth is below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm a strengthening labor market, supporting consumer spending and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to achieve statistical significance (p > 0.05) in predicting 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate cuts implemented during a monetary easing cycle provide immediate liquidity relief, compressing risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months during easing cycles.
